channel.title: NYT > Top Stories
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Trump Demands ‘Unconditional Surrender’ by Iran, Shifting U.S. Objectives Again
item.description: President Trump laid out yet another, more ambitious goal of U.S. military action, one that could extend the war.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Iranians mourning the killing of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, the supreme leader, in Tehran on Thursday night.

item.title: With Iran, Trump Takes the U.S. to War Without the Public’s Support
item.description: In opening a military campaign against Iran, President Trump is the first president in modern times to take the United States to war without the backing of the public.
item.dc:creator: Peter Baker
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump on the USS George H.W. Bush in October. Polling shows public support for striking Iran is below what his predecessors enjoyed initially when they used force overseas.

item.title: For Xi, Trump’s Embrace of War Proves China Needs More Power
item.description: China is learning lessons from the war in Iran. Chief among them, the United States may pose an even greater threat than Beijing thought.
item.dc:creator: David Pierson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Maxim Shemetov/Reuters
item.media:description: Xi Jinping, China’s top leader, during an annual legislative meeting in Beijing on Wednesday.

item.title: Kristi Noem Survived Many Crises. Then She Crossed a Trump Red Line.
item.description: President Trump, who values loyalty, has at times tried to distance himself from his administration’s own actions when they become politically toxic.
item.dc:creator: Zolan Kanno-Youngs and Hamed Aleaziz
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: Kristi Noem testifying before the Senate Judiciary Committee on Tuesday, where her comments led the president to fire her later in the week.

item.title: Justice Dept. Denounces Federal Judges in Fight Against Law Firms
item.description: The Trump administration had signaled earlier this week that it was ready to abandon four executive orders seeking to punish law firms, but abruptly reversed course the next day.
item.dc:creator: Chris Cameron and Jonah E. Bromwich
item.category: Wilmer Cutler Pickering Hale and Dorr LLP
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Justice Department is accusing federal judges of undermining President Trump’s authority.

item.title: Trump Calls On Private Companies to Take On a Bigger Role in Cyber
item.description: In his National Cybersecurity Strategy, President Trump sought an expanded role for private firms in cyberwarfare. He did not take on China or Russia in the document.
item.dc:creator: Adam Sella
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump on Friday released the National Cybersecurity Strategy, which calls for private companies to play a more active role.

item.title: Jesse Jackson Chicago Funeral: Obama, Biden, Clinton to Attend
item.description: The large-scale, public event in Chicago on Friday caps two weeks of memorials for Mr. Jackson, the civil rights leader, who died at 84.
item.dc:creator: Julie Bosman
item.media:credit: Jamie Kelter Davis for The New York Times
item.media:description: Three of the four living former presidents were invited to speak at Rev. Jesse Jackson’s public service in Chicago on Friday.

item.title: New Nonfiction to Read This Spring
item.description: Memoirs from Liza Minnelli and Arsenio Hall; essays from David Sedaris and Jesmyn Ward; plus histories, true crime, biographies and more.
item.media:credit: The New York Times

item.title: New Novels to Read This Spring
item.description: New novels from Tana French, Emma Straub, Ben Lerner, Solvej Balle, Shannon Chakraborty, Tom Perrotta, Elizabeth Strout — and plenty more.
item.media:credit: The New York Times

item.title: Brandon Herrera’s YouTube Gives Democrats More Hope in West Texas Race
item.description: What had been a safe G.O.P. seat was looking more attainable for Democrats after Representative Tony Gonzales bowed out in favor of a hard-right candidate.
item.dc:creator: Lauren McGaughy
item.media:credit: Brenda BazáN/Associated Press
item.media:description: Brandon Herrera had forced a run-off with the incumbent, the Republican Tony Gonzales, before Mr. Gonzales ended his re-election bid.

item.title: Talarico’s Social Media Paper Trail, the Jobs Report and More Politics News
item.description: For some Democrats, generational change comes with a cringey social media past.
item.dc:creator: Katie Glueck
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Tamir Kalifa for The New York Times
item.media:description: James Talarico, the state representative from Texas who is now the Democratic nominee for Senate in the state.

item.title: Vulnerable California Republican Darrell Issa Drops Re-election Bid
item.description: The decisions by Darrell Issa and Kevin Kiley, made newly vulnerable by redistricting, demonstrated the challenges Republicans face in a difficult midterm environment.
item.dc:creator: Kellen Browning
item.media:credit: Valerie Plesch for The New York Times
item.media:description: Representative Darrell Issa has served for 12 terms in the House.

item.title: At Least 4 Dead and 12 Injured as Tornadoes Slam Michigan
item.description: Tornadoes damaged buildings, cut power and injured more than a dozen people in the two states as severe weather swept across the central United States.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Ziegler, Rylee Kirk, Amy Graff and John Yoon

item.title: What to Do When There’s a Tornado Warning
item.description: A tornado warning demands instant action. Here’s what to do if one comes your way, and how you can prepare.
item.dc:creator: Amanda Holpuch
item.media:credit: Joseph Rushmore for The New York Times
item.media:description: Locust Grove, Okla., in 2019. Severe storms in the area brought about several tornado warnings.

item.title: Surfers in Munich (Yes, Munich) Just Want Their Wave Back
item.description: For decades, enthusiasts rode the cresting Eisbach creek in the southern German city, some 200 miles from the nearest coast. But the wave has vanished, prompting arguments about how to restore it.
item.dc:creator: Christopher F. Schuetze and Laetitia Vançon
item.media:credit: Laetitia Vancon for The New York Times

item.title: Top National Symphony Leader Quits in New Blow to Kennedy Center
item.description: The executive director, Jean Davidson, said her departure reflects frustration at the turmoil that has engulfed the arts center.
item.dc:creator: Adam Nagourney
item.category: Kennedy, John F, Center for the Performing Arts
item.media:credit: Caroline Gutman for The New York Times
item.media:description: “It’s no secret that this has been a really hard year,” the executive director of the National Symphony Orchestra, Jean Davidson, said of her decision to leave her post after less than three years.

item.title: Lawmaker Asks Court to Block Trump From Closing Kennedy Center
item.description: The president has said he plans to shut down the center for two years starting this summer for a “complete rebuilding.”
item.dc:creator: Luke Broadwater
item.category: Kennedy, John F, Center for the Performing Arts
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: “President Trump’s own words indicate that he is planning to demolish the existing Kennedy Center building,” says a lawsuit by Representative Joyce Beatty, Democrat of Ohio.

item.title: Russia Gets Silent Treatment in Paralympics Return
item.description: Russia’s flag was paraded at the opening ceremony of the Paralympics for the first time since 2014 prompting a boycott by several nations led by Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Tariq Panja and Josephine de La Bruyère
item.media:credit: Luca Bruno/Associated Press
item.media:description: Two Russian athletes, center, and Russian officials participated in the opening ceremony of the Winter Paralympics in Verona, Italy.

item.title: U.S. Gas Prices Jump Again as Oil Tops $90 for First Time in Years
item.description: The price of a gallon of gasoline hits its highest level in a year and a half on Friday. Surging oil prices suggest that the increases will continue.
item.dc:creator: Emmett Lindner
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Patrick T. Fallon/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images

item.title: The State of U.S. Markets
item.description: Our business reporter Joe Rennison walks us through three charts to help make sense of the wild swings in the markets this week since the attacks on Iran commenced.
item.dc:creator: Joe Rennison, Sutton Raphael, James Surdam and Thomas Vollkommer

item.title: What to know about the jobs report.
item.description: Employers shed 92,000 jobs in February and the unemployment rate rose to 4.4 percent.
item.dc:creator: Sydney Ember

item.title: NASA’s Asteroid-Smashing DART Mission Sped Up Space Rock’s Orbit Around the Sun
item.description: New data about the DART spacecraft’s effects adds evidence that Earth could be defended from future deadly asteroids by diverting their orbits.
item.dc:creator: Katrina Miller
item.media:credit: NASA/Johns Hopkins APL
item.media:description: Dimorphos is about 525 feet around and orbits a larger parent asteroid, Didymos.

item.title: Trump’s Fantasy Is Crashing Down
item.description: The repercussions of his reckless war in Iran are just beginning.
item.dc:creator: Lydia Polgreen
item.media:credit: Photo Illustration by Damon Winter/The New York Times

item.title: I’ve Never Been a Patriot, but Spain Standing Up to Trump Has Made Me One
item.description: There are times when it is clear what it means to stand up for what is right.
item.dc:creator: Paco Cerdà
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Jon Nazca/Reuters

item.title: Mamdani Defends Wife Amid Criticism of Her Support for Palestinian Cause
item.description: Rama Duwaji, Mr. Mamdani’s wife, had liked Instagram posts related to the Hamas-led attack on Israel on Oct. 7, 2023.
item.dc:creator: Jeffery C. Mays and Sally Goldenberg
item.category: War Crimes, Genocide and Crimes Against Humanity
item.media:credit: Amir Hamja for The New York Times
item.media:description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani said his wife, Rama Duwaji, is a “private person” who does not have a responsibility to answer for her thoughts the way that he does.

item.title: Defense Without U.S. Help Is a Live Topic for Canada, Japan and Australia
item.description: The leaders of Japan and Canada are making a unified front on defense cooperation as President Trump raises the pressure over military spending.
item.dc:creator: Ian Austen
item.media:credit: Adrian Wyld/The Canadian Press, via Associated Press
item.media:description: Prime Minister Mark Carney of Canada arriving in Tokyo on Friday as part of a tour that also included Australia.

item.title: Here Is Mexico’s Plan to Keep the Country Safe During the World Cup
item.description: Officials announced a massive deployment of security forces ahead of the world’s largest sporting event after recent violence prompted safety concerns for the millions of fans expected to attend.
item.dc:creator: James Wagner and David Shortell
item.media:credit: Raquel Cunha/Reuters
item.media:description: Banorte Stadium, also known as Azteca Stadium, the venue in Mexico City that will host the opening match of the 2026 World Cup.
